summary:
The agent concludes there is no serovar-specific immunity because *N. gonorrhoeae* uses antigenic variation and active suppression of host immune responses to evade protection. This means prior exposure or infection fails to generate lasting, variant-targeted immunity capable of preventing reinfection with different strains.

The agent determines that no serovar-specific immunity exists due to *Neisseria gonorrhoeae*'s ability to undergo antigenic variation and actively suppress host immune responses. As a result, previous exposures do not lead to durable, strain-specific protective immunity effective against subsequent infections.  

Final answer: \boxed{B}
